div.p: epoll: Each I/O operation requires kernel transition for notification AND execution. io_uring: Shared ring buffers enable batch submission/harvest with single kernel transition. The architectural difference compounds at scale — epoll syscall overhead grows linearly with events, while io_uring grows with batch count (typically 64-256 operations per batch).

ul.li: Total: 1 + 2N syscalls For 64 ready fds: 129 syscalls
Total: 2 syscalls per batch For 64 operations: 2 syscalls
